solutions.solution: There was not much to be looked about beside the body, no murder might have occurred anytime during midnight. There was no evidence of any document or valuables missing. Incidentally, the TV was switched on with its volume very loud. The murderer killed the owner of the TV as a result of a quarrel because the latter was not ready to reduce the volume.

It can be safely assumed that if more and more 'attractive' incentives for the SSIs of rural sector are announced, this would attract more and more people to open SSIs and this in turn would generate more employment. Hence, the inference is definitely true.

It can be safely assumed that nobody can protest against an action which is beneficial to him. Hence, 'the action' of providing incentives to the SSIs can not have the 'effect' of protest from the SSIs. The inference is definitely false.

We can safely assume that free food usually attracts people. Under this assumption we may consider it likely that more parents would send their children to school. Hence, the school attendance will improve. However, this effect is not definite to happen. Maybe, the parents would find it better to send their children to work rather to eat a free meal. Hence, the inference is probably true but not definitely true.

People may not like the idea of reducing the size of bureaucracy but history suggests that mildly harsh decisions do not bring Indian people on the verge of revolution. Under this assumption, therefore, the conclusion appears false. But, it cannot be declared definitely false; after all, anything may happen. Hence, it is probably false.

The passage states that children having 'malnutrition' form one group that shows the 'property' of muscular inefficiency and less intellectual development. Now, children from poor families are exactly similar group because they live under the constant condition of malnutrition because of poverty. Hence, they too will exhibit the same property. Thus, the given inference is definitely true.
